label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the headline "Safina goes from basket case to top of the world." I need to figure out what it implies. First, I'll recall the context from the article. The article mentions that Dinara Safina has had volatile emotions, sometimes being described as "mad as a snake." The headline in the Sydney Morning Herald during the Australian Open says she went from "basket case to top of the world." A "basket case" is an idiom meaning someone who is very nervous or emotionally unstable. So the headline is probably highlighting her emotional journey from being unstable to achieving great success. Looking at the options: A) Safina wanted to top the world when she was a baby in a basket. That doesn't make sense. "Basket case" is an idiom, not literal. B) She cried after winning the Australian Open. The headline doesn't mention crying; it's about her emotional state improving. C) Too nervous at first but gained confidence and won. This fits with the idea of moving from being a basket case (nervous/emotionally unstable) to the top. The article says she made it to the Australian Open final and won a silver medal, so her success would be the "top of the world" part. D) Learnt from
